Title: Effective prediction of biosynthetic pathway genes involved in bioactive polyphyllins in Paris polyphylla

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins are pharmacologically active steroidal saponins.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpOSC2 was experimentally tested but did not produce cycloartenol; instead it increased an uncharacterized triterpene in Nicotiana benthamiana. Because its exact metabolic role in polyphyllin biosynthesis was not established, it is treated as a common core gene rather than a pathway-confirmed gene.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast heterologous expression; Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential source of alternative triterpene scaffold synthesis, but unsuitable for confirmed polyphyllin pathway engineering without further characterization.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; type NA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not increase cycloartenol; may generate an unknown triterpene product.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast assay; Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression; GC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpOSC2 was one of two OSC candidates identified. 2. Unlike PpOSC1, it failed to produce cycloartenol in yeast and Nicotiana assays. 3. It may synthesize an uncharacterized triterpene, so its direct role in polyphyllin biosynthesis remains unresolved.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins are medicinal steroidal saponins with multiple pharmacological activities.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized CYP reported by this and prior work as involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis; this paper did not newly functionally validate it but used it as a positive benchmark candidate ranked 1st.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate mining through phylogeny, WGCNA, expression-metabolite association
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important known pathway CYP for future polyphyllin engineering, but no new functional assay in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously reported to be involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced previous characterization; in this paper supported by candidate ranking via phylogeny/WGCNA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP90G4 was among top predicted candidate CYPs. 2. It ranked 1st among candidate genes. 3. It had been previously reported as involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis and served as validation of the prediction pipeline.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizome and other tissues depending on polyphyllin species

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins are medicinal steroidal saponins with multiple pharmacological activities.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized CYP involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis; not newly assayed in this study.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate mining through phylogeny, WGCNA, expression-metabolite association
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful known upstream oxidation gene for pathway reconstruction with PpOSC1 and glycosyltransferases.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously reported to be involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced previous characterization; in this paper supported by candidate ranking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP90B27 was among narrowed candidate CYP genes. 2. It ranked 3rd in candidate prioritization. 3. Its prior functional assignment supports the effectiveness of the prediction strategy.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizome and other tissues depending on specific polyphyllin

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins are medicinal steroidal saponins with multiple pharmacological activities.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized CYP involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis; not newly functionally verified here. The transcript ID appears inconsistently rendered in the supplied text, so accession is kept general to candidate-gene GenBank range.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate mining through phylogeny, WGCNA, expression-metabolite association
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate module membership not explicitly resolved in text excerpt
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream oxidation gene for reconstructing diosgenin/polyphyllin branch in heterologous systems.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously reported to be involved in polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced previous characterization; in this paper supported by candidate ranking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP72A616 was among the prioritized CYP candidates. 2. It ranked 6th in candidate list. 3. Its previous functional assignment helped validate the integrated prediction pipeline.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizome and other tissues depending on polyphyllin distribution

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cycloartenol production and functions as the cycloartenol synthase required for upstream polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an upstream entry-point gene for metabolic engineering of steroidal sapogenin/polyphyllin production; can be stacked with downstream CYP and UGT genes to reconstruct pathway flux toward diosgenin/polyphyllins.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; yeast heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana; type NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream scaffold-forming step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate identified from tissue transcriptome/WGCNA; exact tissue-high pattern not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Yeast and Nicotiana benthamiana assays both supported cycloartenol formation. The paper states PpOSC1, but PpOSC2 produced an uncharacterized triterpene and was not assigned to polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpOSC1 was one of two intact OSCs identified from full-length transcriptome data. 2. In engineered yeast, PpOSC1 enabled evident cycloartenol production, whereas PpOSC2 did not. 3. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana increased cycloartenol ~3.12-fold, supporting its role as cycloartenol synthase in the polyphyllin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hybrid transcriptomics (Illumina + PacBio); metabolomics; WGCNA; BioProject PRJCA004404
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast heterologous expression with GC-MS; Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins possess anti-inflammatory, vascular protective, hypoglycemic, immunomodulatory, antiparasitic, hypocholesterolemic, antifungal, and antitumor activities.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizome primarily; related metabolites also accumulate in leaf, ovary, petal, fibrous root, and fruit depending on compound

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes C-3 glucosylation of diosgenin to trillin and of pennogenin to floribundasaponin A, thereby promoting polyphyllin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable terminal tailoring enzyme for synthetic biology production of trillin and pennogenin-derived glycosides; can be combined with upstream sapogenin pathway genes for polyphyllin pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays with UDP-glucose and diosgenin or pennogenin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGEX-6p-1 bacterial expression vector; type NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal glycosylation determinant at C-3; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT73 family; PSPG-box-containing plant UGT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predicted from coral co-expression module associated with polyphyllin-related metabolites; exact tissue-high pattern not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the C-3 hydroxyl of diosgenin or pennogenin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows substrate promiscuity but higher catalytic efficiency toward diosgenin than pennogenin. Product identities were supported by HPLC, LC-TOF-MS, and comparison with standards/spectra.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested candidate UGTs, only transcript/33044 showed C-3 glycosyltransferase activity and was named PpUGT73CR1. 2. It converted diosgenin to trillin and pennogenin to floribundasaponin A using UDP-glucose. 3. Kinetic analysis showed higher affinity and catalytic efficiency for diosgenin than for pennogenin.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Trillin from diosgenin; floribundasaponin A from pennogenin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hybrid transcriptomics (Illumina + PacBio); metabolomics; WGCNA; BioProject PRJCA004404
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein expression; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC; LC-TOF-MS; enzyme kinetics; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphyllins and related steroidal saponins are bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, antiparasitic, hypocholesterolemic, antifungal, and antitumor activities.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizome and multiple tissues depending on specific polyphyllin/trillin distribution
